2604.04196.title: Robots Need Some Education: On the complexity of learning in evolutionary robotics
2604.04196.paper_summary: Evolutionary Robotics and Robot Learning are two fields in robotics that aim to automatically optimize robot designs. The key difference between them lies in what is being optimized and the time scale involved. Evolutionary Robotics is a field that applies evolutionary computation techniques to evolve the morphologies or controllers, or both. Robot Learning, on the other hand, involves any learning technique aimed at optimizing a robot's controller in a given morphology. In terms of time scales, evolution occurs across multiple generations, whereas learning takes place within the `lifespan' of an individual robot. Integrating Robot Learning with Evolutionary Robotics requires the careful design of suitable learning algorithms in the context of evolutionary robotics. The effects of introducing learning into the evolutionary process are not well-understood and can thus be tricky. This thesis investigates these intricacies and presents several learning algorithms developed for an Evolutionary Robotics context.
2604.04196.paper_summary_zh: 進化機器人學和機器人學習是機器人領域中的兩個分支，旨在自動優化機器人設計。<lb/>它們之間的主要區別在於優化的對象和涉及的時間尺度。<lb/>進化機器人學是一個應用進化計算技術來進化形態或控制器，或兩者兼具的領域。<lb/>另一方面，機器人學習涉及任何旨在優化特定形態下機器人控制器的學習技術。<lb/>在時間尺度方面，進化發生在多代之間，而學習則發生在單個機器人的「壽命」內。<lb/>將機器人學習與進化機器人學結合需要在進化機器人學的背景下仔細設計合適的學習算法。<lb/>將學習引入進化過程的影響尚不完全了解，因此可能會很棘手。<lb/>本論文探討了這些複雜性，並提出了幾種為進化機器人學背景開發的學習算法。
2604.04196.author: Fuda van Diggelen et.al.
